## 2025-02-14 - Interactive elements generated by custom DOM scripts need explicit accessibility
**Learning:** When using Obsidian DOM helpers (`createEl`, `createDiv`), complex interactive elements (like custom task titles, stat cards, or header wrappers configured with `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"`) often lack default `:focus-visible` styles, making keyboard navigation difficult to track. Additionally, explicitly created form elements like `<select>` dropdowns may end up disconnected from their visual `<label>` counterparts for screen readers if `id`/`for` properties aren't wired up, requiring `aria-label` attributes to ensure programmatic accessibility.
**Action:** When creating custom interactive DOM components, always explicitly configure `:focus-visible` styles in the CSS and ensure `aria-label`s are applied to natively accessible elements (like dropdowns or buttons) to bridge any gaps in visual-to-programmatic association.
